message: Temperature-sensitive cargo cannot be assessed confidently without a required temperature range.
Perishable cargo requires exact commodity because handling, labels, and shelf-life differ.
Live animal cargo requires species and container/crate details before route suitability can be assessed.
Heavy/outsized cargo cannot be assessed without piece dimensions and piece weight.
Special cargo requires aircraft/ULD compatibility and loading constraints before acceptance.
Security-sensitive cargo needs screening/secure supply-chain status before passenger/all-cargo suitability is assessed.
Valuable/vulnerable cargo should not receive high confidence without secure storage and chain-of-custody checks.
Urgent medical cargo needs hard deadline and 24-hour contact details.
Human remains require country documents and airline acceptance validation.
Medical specimens/infectious substances require classification and packaging checks, with Block A DG cross-check if applicable.
